templates.description: Large animated number over the unified dark pastel watercolor background with minimal supporting context.
templates.whenToUse: Use for a single memorable statistic, percentage, dollar amount, study result, or surprising quantified claim that should feel premium and focused.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use <arg name="value">...</arg> for the primary statistic and <arg name="title">...</arg> for the short supporting context.
Keep the title compact; this template is for one dominant quantified idea, not a multi-line explanation.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-4" label="Key statistic" start="8.00" end="12.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="stat_reveal">
    <timing item="value" at="8.30" />
    <timing item="title" at="8.90" />
    <arg name="value">73%</arg>
    <arg name="title">people notice the change</arg>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Make sure that the end time of the visual using this motion graphic template is at least 3 seconds after the last animation timing timestamp, so readers have time to read and digest the motion graphic.

fields.name: value
fields.label: Main value
fields.description: The large central statistic, number, percentage, or short quantified phrase that should be the visual's main focus.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: The title for the slide

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Soft accent as the main stat resolves
Subtle tick as supporting title text appears

templates.whenToUse: Use when comparing 2–4 different things using a bar chart.
templates.additionalUsageInstructions: Make the bars that you want to emphasize the most come last (e.g. if you're emphasizing that one thing was rated higher, make it the last bar, similarly, if you're emphasizing it was rated lower, put it last).
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use repeated <item label="..." value="..." displayValue="..." animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds" /> entries for the data field.
Use 2-5 items. The numeric value controls bar height; displayValue is what viewers read.
Put reveal timings on each bar's animateIn attribute; do not use one shared <timing item="data" ... /> for the whole data set.
If one bar is the key reveal, put it later in the item order so its animation lands last.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-5" label="Outcome comparison" start="12.00" end="18.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="bar_chart">
    <timing item="title" at="12.20" />
    <arg name="title">What changed most</arg>
    <item label="Before" value="35" displayValue="35%" animateIn="12.90" />
    <item label="After" value="82" displayValue="82%" animateIn="13.70" />
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Ideally, leave up to 3 seconds after the last bar starts animating-in, so viewers have time to digest the information.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: Compact chart headline that tells viewers what the bars compare. Keep it short enough to read before the bars animate.

fields.name: data
fields.label: Data points
fields.description: Use 2-5 <item label="Category" value="68" displayValue="68%" /> entries. The numeric value controls bar height; displayValue is the readable label shown with the bar.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Sparse tick as each bar animates in

templates.whenToUse: Use when visually comparing 2-4 items visually using a classic pie chart.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use repeated <item label="..." value="..." displayValue="..." animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds" /> entries for the data field.
Values should be positive parts of a whole. displayValue should usually be a compact percent or share label.
Put reveal timings on each slice item's animateIn attribute; do not use one shared <timing item="data" ... /> for the whole pie.
Use 2-5 slices and keep labels short enough for the legend.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-5" label="Time split" start="12.00" end="18.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="pie_chart">
    <timing item="title" at="12.20" />
    <arg name="title">Where time goes</arg>
    <item label="Practice" value="50" displayValue="50%" animateIn="12.85" />
    <item label="Recovery" value="30" displayValue="30%" animateIn="13.35" />
    <item label="Setup" value="20" displayValue="20%" animateIn="13.85" />
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Ideally, leave up to 3 seconds after that last pi chart slice starts animating-in, so viewers have time to digest the information.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: Compact chart headline that explains what the slices add up to or compare.

fields.name: data
fields.label: Pie slices
fields.description: Use <item label="Category" value="35" displayValue="35%" />. Positive values are normalized into the full pie.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Light tick as each pie slice appears

templates.description: Simple animated line chart to show something increases or decreases
templates.whenToUse: Use when you want to show that something increases/improves or decreases/worsens.
templates.additionalUsageInstructions: Leave the "valueLabel" and "units" blank if there's no clear metric to display. Do not use a "valueLabel" or "units" if there's no clear metric to change.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Set <arg name="direction">increase</arg> for an up/right trend or <arg name="direction">decrease</arg> for a down/right trend.
Optional dataSeries points should match the chosen direction.
Use <timing item="title" ... /> for the headline and <timing item="chart" ... /> for the chart line/counter reveal.
To show units on the moving counter, set <arg name="valueLabel">90</arg> and optional <arg name="units">homes</arg>; the renderer appends the unit text throughout the count-up.
Leave valueLabel and units blank when there is no clear metric to display.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-6" label="Trend rising" start="18.00" end="24.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="line_growth_chart">
    <timing item="title" at="18.15" />
    <timing item="chart" at="18.80" />
    <arg name="title">Growth trend</arg>
    <arg name="direction">increase</arg>
    <arg name="startLabel">Start</arg>
    <arg name="endLabel">Now</arg>
    <arg name="valueLabel">90</arg>
    <arg name="units">homes</arg>
    <item label="Start" value="22" displayValue="22" />
    <item label="Now" value="90" displayValue="90" />
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Leave around 4 seconds after the chart line starts animating in, so that the viewer can digest the chart

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: Short headline for the trend. Name the metric or change the line represents.

fields.name: direction
fields.label: Direction
fields.description: Exactly one of: increase, decrease. increase animates up/right; decrease animates down/right.

fields.name: startLabel
fields.label: Start label
fields.description: Small label for the left/start side of the chart, such as Before, Week 1, Baseline, or Start.

fields.name: endLabel
fields.label: End label
fields.description: Small label for the right/final side of the chart, such as After, Now, Week 4, or Result.

fields.name: valueLabel
fields.label: Final value label
fields.description: Optional. When present, it counts up from 0 and follows the arrowhead.

fields.name: units
fields.label: Counter units
fields.description: Optional unit text appended to the animated value, such as homes, leads, dollars, or clients. Example: valueLabel 90 with units homes renders as 90 homes while counting up.

fields.name: data
fields.label: Trend points
fields.description: Optional <item label="Week 1" value="22" displayValue="22" /> points. Use rising values for increase and falling values for decrease.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Quiet tick as the chart frame starts drawing
Soft payoff accent when the trend line reaches its final value

templates.description: Minimal two-column comparison over the unified dark pastel watercolor background with no panels or card boxes.
templates.whenToUse: Use for transformations, posture/routine contrasts, old-vs-new workflow, or mistake-vs-fix moments.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use beforeLabel/afterLabel for the small comparison labels and before/after for the main copy.
Keep before and after copy parallel so the visual reads as one clear transformation.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-7" label="Before and after" start="24.00" end="30.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="comparison_before_after">
    <timing item="before" at="24.40" />
    <timing item="after" at="26.10" />
    <arg name="beforeLabel">Before</arg>
    <arg name="afterLabel">After</arg>
    <arg name="before">Tension stacked under the chin</arg>
    <arg name="after">Neck long, jawline reads cleaner</arg>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Usually around 7 to 8 seconds: give the before side time to read first, then reveal the after side with a short pause before the visual ends.

fields.name: beforeLabel
fields.label: Before label
fields.description: Short label for the left/problem state. Use labels like Before, Old way, Mistake, or Without.

fields.name: afterLabel
fields.label: After label
fields.description: Short label for the right/improved state. Use labels like After, New way, Fix, or With.

fields.name: before
fields.label: Before copy
fields.description: Concise text describing the initial, weaker, risky, or less desirable state. Keep it parallel with the after copy.

fields.name: after
fields.label: After copy
fields.description: Concise text describing the improved, corrected, or more desirable state. Keep it parallel with the before copy.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Subtle tick as the before side appears
Soft accent as the after side lands

templates.description: A classic timeline displaying a sequence of items.
templates.whenToUse: Use to visually display a sequence of events, actions, or any other sequence of items that makes sense to represent in a timeline.
templates.additionalUsageInstructions: Timeline step labels should all belong to the same semantic category of abstraction. Do not mix label types in one timeline, such as using a date for one step label and a percentage for another step label.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Optionally add <arg name="title">...</arg> for a left-aligned title above the timeline items.
Use repeated <step label="custom left label" animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds">step text</step> entries.
Put reveal timings on each timeline step's animateIn attribute; do not use one shared <timing item="steps" ... /> for the whole timeline.
Omit label only when you want the renderer to auto-label steps as 01, 02, 03.
Keep all step labels in the same semantic category, such as all dates, all phases, or all percentages.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-8" label="Three-step timeline" start="30.00" end="38.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="timeline">
    <arg name="title">Thirty-day ramp</arg>
    <step label="DAY 1" animateIn="30.50">Setup</step>
    <step label="DAY 7" animateIn="32.10">Signal</step>
    <step label="DAY 30" animateIn="34.00">Visible change</step>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Leave about 3 seconds after the last timeline step starts animating-in so viewers have time to digest the timeline.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: Optional left-aligned title shown above the timeline items. Leave blank when the timeline steps speak for themselves.

fields.name: steps
fields.label: Timeline steps
fields.description: Array of { label, text, animateIn } objects. Legacy string arrays still work and auto-label as 01, 02, 03.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Tick as each timeline step activates

templates.description: Vertically stacked cause-effect relationship diagram. Displays cause text, a thick downward arrow, and effect text without boxes around the text.
templates.whenToUse: Use when explaining mechanisms, causal relationships, inputs that drive outcomes, or why one action creates a visible result.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use <arg name="cause">...</arg> for the input or mechanism and <arg name="effect">...</arg> for the outcome.
Use <timing item="cause" ... />, <timing item="arrow" ... />, and <timing item="effect" ... /> when those three beats should reveal separately.
Keep each side short and concrete so the downward causal relationship is obvious.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-9" label="Cause and effect" start="38.00" end="44.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="cause_effect">
    <timing item="cause" at="38.30" />
    <timing item="arrow" at="39.25" />
    <timing item="effect" at="40.10" />
    <arg name="cause">Small daily tension</arg>
    <arg name="effect">Jaw and neck read tighter</arg>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Leave about 3 seconds after the "effect" starting animating in, so viewers have time to digest the diagram.

fields.name: cause
fields.label: Cause
fields.description: The input, habit, condition, or mechanism that starts the causal chain. Keep it short and concrete.

fields.name: effect
fields.label: Effect
fields.description: The outcome or visible consequence produced by the cause. Phrase it as the result viewers should remember.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Light tick as the cause card enters
Soft downward motion accent as the arrow grows
Soft impact as the effect card lands

templates.description: Full-screen caption wall that reveals one continuous spoken-text flow using forced-alignment word timing, inline size tags, and an active-word pop.
templates.whenToUse: Use for retention-heavy moments where the exact narrated words should become the full-frame visual instead of ordinary bottom captions or a generated image.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use one <arg name="text">...</arg> containing the exact spoken narration text for this visual, including punctuation.
Do not split the text into separate <line> rows; the renderer lays it out as one wrapping caption wall.
Regular words need no tag. Wrap individual emphasized words or phrases in <large>...</large> or <extraLarge>...</extraLarge>.
Words that have not been spoken yet are hidden, not dimmed; word reveal/highlighting follows forced alignment.
This template replaces ordinary bottom captions for that visual and should not be paired with normal captions.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-10" label="Caption wall emphasis" start="44.00" end="49.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="caption_word_wall">
    <arg name="text">most people miss <large>this part,</large> because <extraLarge>the words</extraLarge> become the visual.</arg>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Match the exact spoken narration range for the caption wall. The start/end times should cover only the words represented by the configured text.

previewArgs.text: most people miss <large>this part,</large> because <extraLarge>the words</extraLarge> become the visual.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Caption text
fields.description: Exact spoken narration text for this visual, including punctuation. Use inline <large>...</large> or <extraLarge>...</extraLarge> tags around individual words or phrases to enlarge them.

templates.description: Animated ranking with oversized rank numbers and podium-like rows over the unified dark pastel watercolor background.
templates.whenToUse: Use for top-3/top-5 lists, ranked mistakes, priorities, symptoms, channels, exercises, or results where ranking order matters.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use repeated <step label="01" animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds">ranked item</step> entries. Optional labels are rank markers; omit them for 01, 02, 03 auto-labels.
Put reveal timings on each ranked item's animateIn attribute; do not use one shared <timing item="items" ... /> for the whole list.
For split multi-visual sequences, set <arg name="startIndex">2</arg> to render earlier ranks already present and animate from rank 2.
Set <arg name="futureItemsMode">hidden</arg> or <arg name="futureItemsMode">blurred</arg> to control unrevealed later ranks.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-11" label="Ranked priorities" start="49.00" end="56.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="ranked_podium">
    <arg name="startIndex">1</arg>
    <arg name="futureItemsMode">hidden</arg>
    <step label="01" animateIn="49.40">Most visible change</step>
    <step label="02" animateIn="50.25">Faster feedback</step>
    <step label="03" animateIn="51.10">Cleaner routine</step>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Around 1 second per ranking. For multi-visual sequences, set startIndex to the first rank that should animate in this visual.

fields.name: items
fields.label: Ranked items
fields.description: Use ordered <step> entries. Labels are rank markers and auto-label as 01, 02, 03 when omitted.

fields.name: startIndex
fields.label: Start rank
fields.description: 1-based rank to animate first. Earlier ranks render already present at time 0 for split multi-visual sequences.

fields.name: futureItemsMode
fields.label: Future rank state
fields.description: hidden hides unrevealed future ranks; blurred shows muted ghost rows before they animate/unblur.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Tick as each ranked row animates in

templates.description: Animated list with selectable checklist, numbered, or bulleted markers, concise item copy, and optional future-item ghosting.
templates.whenToUse: Use for routines, protocols, decisions, action lists, unordered points, or ordered items that should appear as a clean list.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Optionally add <arg name="title">...</arg> for a left-aligned title above the list items.
Set <arg name="listType">checklist</arg>, <arg name="listType">numbered</arg>, or <arg name="listType">bulleted</arg> to choose the marker style.
Use repeated <step animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds">...</step> entries for list items. Labels are ignored by this template.
Put reveal timings on each list step's animateIn attribute; do not use one shared <timing item="items" ... /> for the whole list.
For split multi-visual sequences, set <arg name="startIndex">2</arg> to render earlier items already present and animate from item 2.
Set <arg name="futureItemsMode">hidden</arg> or <arg name="futureItemsMode">blurred</arg> to control unrevealed later items.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-12" label="Routine list" start="56.00" end="63.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="list">
    <arg name="title">Daily routine</arg>
    <arg name="listType">checklist</arg>
    <arg name="startIndex">1</arg>
    <arg name="futureItemsMode">hidden</arg>
    <step animateIn="56.40">Set the baseline</step>
    <step animateIn="57.45">Make the small adjustment</step>
    <step animateIn="58.55">Repeat it daily</step>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Around 1 second per list item. For multi-visual sequences, set startIndex to the first item that should animate in this visual.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: Optional left-aligned title shown above the list items. Leave blank when the list does not need a heading.

fields.name: listType
fields.label: List type
fields.description: Exactly one of: checklist, numbered, bulleted. checklist renders checkboxes; numbered renders 1., 2., 3.; bulleted renders bullet points.

fields.name: items
fields.label: List items
fields.description: Use <step animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds">copy</step> entries. Labels are ignored; listType controls whether markers are checkboxes, numbers, or bullets.

fields.name: startIndex
fields.label: Start item
fields.description: 1-based item to animate first. Earlier items render already present at time 0 for split multi-visual sequences.

fields.name: futureItemsMode
fields.label: Future item state
fields.description: hidden hides unrevealed future items; blurred shows muted ghost rows before they animate in.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Tick as each list item appears

templates.description: Animated metric scorecard with rows, values, and compact progress bars.
templates.whenToUse: Use for comparing different metrics, or showing before/after metrics. Can also be used to show "fake metrics" (leave "displayValue" blank) to simply compare things visually using "progress bar"-like visuals.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use <arg name="title">...</arg> plus repeated <item label="..." value="..." displayValue="..." animateIn="absolute_video_timestamp_seconds" /> score rows.
Values are normalized against the largest row, so keep numeric values comparable.
Put reveal timings on each score row's animateIn attribute; do not use one shared <timing item="data" ... /> for the whole scorecard.
Use displayValue for the readable score, such as 82/100, High, or 4.5x.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-13" label="Scorecard" start="63.00" end="69.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="scorecard">
    <timing item="title" at="63.20" />
    <arg name="title">Scorecard</arg>
    <item label="Clarity" value="82" displayValue="82/100" animateIn="63.90" />
    <item label="Consistency" value="68" displayValue="68/100" animateIn="64.65" />
    <item label="Effort" value="91" displayValue="91/100" animateIn="65.40" />
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Leave about 3 seconds after the last item starts animating-in, so viewers have time to digest the visual.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Title
fields.description: Short title naming what is being scored or compared across the rows.

fields.name: data
fields.label: Score rows
fields.description: Use <item label="Metric" value="82" displayValue="82/100" />. Values are normalized against the largest row.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Tick as each scorecard row and bar appears

templates.description: Formal research-paper visual with a paper sheet, journal/year cues, citation mark, compressed title, and one short key finding.
templates.whenToUse: Use when citing a study, paper, review, clinical trial, author group, journal, or named research result. Keep text minimal and let the paper-like structure carry authority.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use source, year, title, and finding args to create a compact citation-style card.
Use <timing item="paper" ... />, <timing item="source" ... />, <timing item="title" ... />, and <timing item="finding" ... /> when those citation beats should reveal separately.
Keep the finding to one plain-English takeaway; do not write a full abstract.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-14" label="Study finding" start="69.00" end="75.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="research_paper_card">
    <timing item="paper" at="69.15" />
    <timing item="source" at="69.25" />
    <timing item="title" at="69.75" />
    <timing item="finding" at="70.60" />
    <arg name="source">Journal of Applied Research</arg>
    <arg name="year">2024</arg>
    <arg name="title">Daily posture cues changed perceived jawline definition</arg>
    <arg name="finding">The visible difference came from consistency, not intensity.</arg>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Usually 5-7 seconds: reveal the paper/source first, then a single key finding. Avoid long abstracts or multi-sentence explanations.

previewArgs.title: Posture cues and visible jawline definition

fields.name: source
fields.label: Source / journal
fields.description: Short journal, author group, or source name.

fields.name: year
fields.label: Year
fields.description: Short year or date cue.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Paper title
fields.description: Keep this short; the renderer compresses it into a formal title block.

fields.name: finding
fields.label: Key finding
fields.description: One concise finding, ideally under 9 words.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Subtle accent as the key finding block appears

templates.description: Minimal good/bad indicator card with one indicator type and one short supporting line. Good uses a green accent; bad uses a red accent.
templates.whenToUse: Use to highlight that something is “good” or “bad” or when instructing the viewer to do something or to not-do/stop doing something. Use this template only when there's a single good/bad thing you want to communicate -- don't use it if you want to communicate more than one thing.
templates.additionalUsageInstructions: Only communicate one thing/idea/point in the indicator text. Don't try to communicate multiple things/ideas/points. If you need to communicate multiple things/ideas/points using the motion graphic template, then consider creating multiple different motion graphics visuals using this template, or using a different visual altogether.
templates.xmlInstructions:
Use exactly <arg name="indicatorType">good</arg> or <arg name="indicatorType">bad</arg>.
Use one <arg name="text">...</arg> field for the rule, habit, mistake, warning, or recommendation.
Communicate one idea only. If there are multiple ideas, use multiple visuals or another template. This text arg should be one complete sentence with the period omitted; do not use partial phrases or multiple sentences.
templates.exampleXml:
<visual id="visual-15" label="Good habit" start="75.00" end="80.00" visualType="motion_graphic">
  <motionGraphic templateId="good-bad-indicator">
    <timing item="text" at="75.35" />
    <arg name="indicatorType">good</arg>
    <arg name="text">Lift from the lower lid</arg>
  </motionGraphic>
</visual>
templates.durationGuidance: Leave about 3 seconds after the text animates in.

fields.name: indicatorType
fields.label: Indicator type
fields.description: Exactly one of: good, bad. Use good for positive/recommended items; use bad for negative/risky items.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Indicator text
fields.description: Short rule, habit, mistake, warning, or recommendation that the good/bad indicator should emphasize.

deterministicSoundEffects.description: Tick as the good/bad icon appears
Soft confirmation accent as the rule underline resolves
